distribution_independence.finding: Distribution type (normal, student_t, laplace, uniform) has no impact on quality scores
distribution_independence.implication: The specific distribution of the special horse performance noise doesn't matter for mapping quality

scale_robustness.finding: Scale parameter (0.5, 1.0, 1.5) has no impact on top configurations
scale_robustness.implication: The variance of the special horse performance distribution is not critical

optimal_base_ability.finding: Base ability +0.5 dominates all top 10 configurations
optimal_base_ability.implication: Moderate special horse advantage creates optimal balance

adaptive_mode_failure.finding: Fixed mode outperforms mean_adaptive and position_adaptive
adaptive_mode_failure.implication: Adaptive strategies don't improve mapping quality with fixed sigmoids

coverage_bottleneck.finding: Coverage score (0.409) limits overall performance despite excellent symmetry (0.964)
coverage_bottleneck.implication: Future optimization should focus on improving simplex coverage

top_configurations.note: Top 10 results show identical scores across different distribution types, confirming distribution independence

research_implications.parameter_focus: Future studies should focus on sigmoid parameter optimization rather than special horse distribution details
research_implications.coverage_optimization: The main limitation is coverage (0.409) - need strategies to improve simplex coverage
research_implications.computational_efficiency: Since distribution type doesn't matter, can use simple normal distribution for efficiency
research_implications.base_ability_tuning: The +0.5 base ability appears to be a global optimum for this sigmoid configuration
